title: Lovalingo
description: A translation tool for vibe coders using Lovable, Replit, Claude Code, and modern web builders.
body:
Lovalingo is a translation tool for vibe coders: people building web pages fast with Lovable, Replit, Claude Code, and other modern AI-first builders.

The problem is simple. Vibe-coded apps can get from idea to live page quickly, but translation is still annoying once the site has multiple routes, components, SEO pages, and product copy. Lovalingo is built to make those pages multilingual without turning the project into a manual i18n cleanup job.

## What it does

- Helps translate web pages built with Lovable, Replit, Claude Code, and React or Next.js stacks.
- Turns fast-built pages into multilingual surfaces without making the builder rewrite everything by hand.
- Supports localization basics like translated copy, routes, SEO pages, canonicals, sitemaps, and public route coverage.
- Gives builders an operational layer for projects, content, users, and translation workflows.

## Recent work from local commits

The recent commits split across the package and the web surface. The package side clarified manifest coverage prompts, documented manifest maintenance for agents, and released `@lovalingo/lovalingo` 0.5.29.

The web side focused on route coverage, language-switcher recovery, static-page bootstrap safety, locale fallback, SEO/GEO surfaces, Google verification, sitemap validation, priority use-case SEO, extractable answers, and a restored public route set.

## What changed in the story

Lovalingo is now framed less as a generic translation button and more as infrastructure for multilingual sites: route discovery, manifest maintenance, public SEO pages, localized paths, and agent-readable surfaces all have to line up.

## Public boundary

This entry describes public package, website, SEO, and route work. It does not expose private customer translations, API keys, CMS credentials, or analytics account data.
